Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for all outstanding invoices related to the April relocation clients using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria referencing "Canopy" or "Mosaic" or "GreenStack" or "PivotPoint" or "NorthWind" or similar broad criteria, possibly also searching by date range or fetching all). The agent needs to find the April relocation invoices and their payment statuses, specifically: Canopy Health INV-2026-0401 ($9,210, with $2,650 in pending surcharges), Vectral Systems INV-2026-0410 ($15,900), GreenStack Energy INV-2026-0418 ($9,440), PivotPoint Labs INV-2026-0315 ($10,650, with $3,610 in surcharges), Mosaic Robotics INV-2026-0411 ($9,010 covering all three April moves, Okoye, Hartley, Tanaka, with $2,450 in pending surcharges: $1,200 custom crating for Okoye, $800 hazmat for Tanaka, $450 interim storage for Tanaka), NorthWind Technologies INV-2026-0412 ($14,700), and BrightLoop invoices. The agent should identify which invoices are paid, unpaid, or have surcharge line items still marked as pending client approval.
OE 2: Search CRM deals for each of the five April relocation clients mentioned in the prompt using crm_search_deals (dealname or company_id for Canopy, Mosaic, GreenStack, PivotPoint, NorthWind). The agent needs to find all active deals and their stages to cross-reference against invoice data. Key findings include: NorthWind's deal (deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2, $14,700) is still at "contractSent" stage. The Mosaic Okoye deal (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) has a RISK flag for the prototype damage claim. Canopy has three separate April surcharge-bearing deals (Kovacs $3,850, Webb $3,200, Okonkwo $2,160) all at "inprogress" stage. GreenStack's April deal (deal_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes, $9,440) is at "inprogress". PivotPoint's April dual relocation deal (deal_pivot_april_2026_dual, $10,650) is at "inprogress".
OE 3: Search Airtable Relocations table using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations") filtered by each client company to verify the status of April moves and identify which have special requirements with surcharges. The agent should find: Canopy has 5 In Progress April relocations (Dominguez, Okafor, Kovacs, Webb, Okonkwo), of which 3 have special requirements tied to pending surcharges on INV-2026-0401, Kovacs (international cross-border, customs broker surcharge estimated at $700-$900 in Airtable, invoiced as $850 on INV-2026-0401), Webb (split-vendor cold-chain shipment for cryogenic lab equipment, no dollar surcharge stated in Airtable but invoiced as $1,200 on INV-2026-0401), and Okonkwo (temporary storage gap with quote pending in Airtable, invoiced as $600 on INV-2026-0401); GreenStack has 3 In Progress April relocations (Herrera, Zhao, Reyes), of which 2 have surcharge-related special requirements, Zhao (Class 9 hazmat lithium-ion battery modules, split shipment, interim storage gap Apr 17-28) and Reyes (900-lb overweight solar test rig, delivery constrained by Seattle office shutdown Apr 14-18); Mosaic has 3 April moves, Okoye (Completed, $1,200 custom crating surcharge for prototypes per Airtable Special Requirements), Hartley (In Progress, no surcharge), Tanaka (In Progress, $800 hazmat handling surcharge + $450 interim storage surcharge per Airtable Special Requirements); PivotPoint has 3 In Progress April relocations (Greenwald, Okonjo, Castaneda), of which 2 have surcharges, Okonjo (H-1B visa complication, 5th-floor Victorian walkup, $500 walkup surcharge per Airtable), Castaneda (CNC mill ~600 lbs, vibration-dampened custom crating, climate-controlled freight, 16-day storage gap, cargo insurance; combined surcharges $3,110 per INV-2026-0315: $1,200 custom crating + $850 climate freight + $640 interim storage + $420 cargo insurance rider, total PivotPoint surcharges $3,610); NorthWind has 2 In Progress April moves (Raj Patel, Class 9 hazmat/chain-of-custody for server lab; Emilia Cruz, Steinway piano requiring specialty movers).
OE 4: Search emails for surcharge approval correspondence using search_emails (query: "surcharge approval" or "pending approval" or "Patricia Langford" or "Sam Delgado" or "Derek Hollis" or "Tamara Byrd" or "Pam Kowalski" or similar). The agent needs to discover whether any of the five clients have already responded to approve or reject the pending surcharges on their April invoices. Key email discoveries include Hana Kim's internal invoice posts flagging non-standard surcharges pending client approval for GreenStack (INV-2026-0418) and Mosaic (INV-2026-0411). The Canopy surcharge approval flag is found via CRM engagements: the agent should also search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids for Canopy, or broad search) to find the structured surcharge approval flag (eng_canopy_surcharge_approval_flag_001), which details three surcharge line items totaling $2,650 on INV-2026-0401 that require explicit client approval from Patricia Langford: $850 customs broker (Kovacs), $1,200 cold-chain freight (Webb), $600 temporary storage (Okonkwo). The deadline for approval is April 7, and vendor commitments cannot be finalized until Patricia approves. The agent needs to determine whether Patricia has responded, based on the universe data, no approval response from Patricia exists.
OE 5: Search emails for the Heartland dispute thread using search_emails (query: "Heartland dispute" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or "Jake Loomis" or "formal dispute" or "overbilling" or similar). The agent needs to find TWO emails in this thread: (A) Marcus's formal dispute letter sent to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com (CC: chloe.vance@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com) contesting 3 of 8 moves ($4,800 overbilling) on invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800); AND (B) Jake Loomis's reply (email_jake_reply_heartland_dispute), which is the universe edit in the changelog. Jake's reply CHANGES the financial picture: he accepts removal of Marchetti ($1,600) and Osei ($1,600) but CONTESTS the Venkatesh charge ($1,600), citing that Heartland's crew was dispatched to Houston on March 7, arrived at 8:15 AM, and was turned away by Swift Relocations, no cancellation notice was provided within the 48-hour SLA (Section 4.2 of the MSA). Heartland's revised offer is $9,600 (original $12,800 minus $3,200 credit for Marchetti and Osei only). This means the disputed amount drops from $4,800 to potentially $1,600 (the Venkatesh line remains contested), and the hold amount should be adjusted from $12,800.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria referencing vendor "Heartland" or similar) to find the Heartland Q1 bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042) and verify its current status. The agent should discover: Balance of $12,800, full payment hold in place. The agent needs to reconcile this against Jake's reply, if Heartland accepts $3,200 credit and MoveOps accepts Heartland's position on Venkatesh, the correct payable would be $9,600; if MoveOps continues to contest, the disputed amount is $1,600 (Venkatesh line only).
OE 7: Search Slack for internal discussions about surcharge approvals, invoice status, and Heartland dispute using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "surcharge" or "pending approval" or "Heartland" or "invoice" or similar, filter_in_channel: #finance or #operations). The agent should find: Hana Kim's invoice posts in #finance with surcharge breakdowns for each client, Marcus's comments on the surcharge approval process, and any operational updates from Chloe about vendor commitments that depend on surcharge approvals.
OE 8: Look up email addresses for Alejandro Fuentes and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro" or "Fuentes") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe" or "Vance") to get their email addresses for the required outbound communications: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 9: Send an email (via send_email) to alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com from marcus.thorne@moveops.com with a subject related to April invoice/margin review (or similar). The email body must include: (a) a summary of all outstanding April relocation invoices and their surcharge statuses across Canopy ($9,210 with $2,650 pending surcharges), GreenStack ($9,440 with $4,400 in total surcharges: $1,800 hazmat transport for Zhao, $1,650 pending interim hazmat storage for Zhao awaiting Derek Hollis approval, $950 overweight equipment for Reyes), PivotPoint ($10,650 with $3,610 in surcharges: $500 walkup for Okonjo + $3,110 for Castañeda's CNC mill), and Mosaic (INV-2026-0411, $9,010 across 3 moves with $2,450 in pending surcharges on Okoye and Tanaka); (b) any discrepancies between invoiced amounts and deal values that could affect margin calculations; (c) the Heartland dispute resolution status, specifically that the revised amount is $9,600, not $8,000, because the Venkatesh line ($1,600) remains contested by Heartland (affects vendor cost assumptions in margin models); (d) any items that Alejandro needs to flag or re-model for accurate margin reporting.
OE 10: Send an email (via send_email) to chloe.vance@moveops.com from marcus.thorne@moveops.com with a subject related to unresolved vendor items for April close-out (or similar). The email body must include: (a) the Heartland dispute update, Jake Loomis replied, accepts $3,200 credit (Marchetti and Osei) but contests the Venkatesh $1,600 charge citing no cancellation notice and 48-hour SLA (Section 4.2 MSA), with attached dispatch/GPS evidence; (b) Heartland's revised invoice offer of $9,600; (c) any vendor commitments that are blocked pending surcharge approvals from clients (e.g., if Swift or BorderEase engagements depend on client sign-off that hasn't come); (d) request for Chloe's input on how to handle the Venkatesh dispute (whether to accept Heartland's position or escalate further).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

This clearly communicates that the remaining contested amount is $1,600 (Venkatesh only) and that $3,200 of the original dispute has been resolved.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly identifies 'The Venkatesh $1,600 is the sticking point' as the only remaining contested charge, and the table shows Marchetti and Osei as ACCEPTED, making clear that $3,200 was resolved and only $1,600 (Venkatesh) remains disputed.
✓Run #3The final response and messages clearly state that $3,200 credit is confirmed (Marchetti + Osei removed) and only the $1,600 Venkatesh charge remains contested. The response says 'Net result: $3,200 credit confirmed. Revised invoice = $9,600. The $1,600 Venkatesh line is the remaining sticking point.'
